tuning: E A D G B E

pro_brother.version_description: Listening closely to the recording it sounds as if he actually plays it in C, but tunes slightly sharp so its halfway in between C and C#.

content:
I tabbed out this version: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J1oU9_hy3mA
But often when they play it live now, they play it a half step down.

Pretty easy chords, but with the C#m you have to play a bit on different positions, so
it doesn't sound repetitive. And play funky!!


[tab][Intro]     [ch]C#m7[/ch]         [ch]F#7[/ch]   
        ||: //// //// | //// //// :|| 4X[/tab]
1x Keys and drums
2x Voice (Ohh, what a night)
3X Add bass
4X Add brass


[Chorus]
[tab][ch]C#m7[/ch]
Oh yes it's ladies night[/tab]
[tab][ch]F#7[/ch]
And the feeling's right[/tab]
[tab][ch]C#m7[/ch]
Oh yes it's ladies night[/tab]
[tab][ch]F#7[/ch]
Oh what a night (oh what a night)[/tab]
(Repeat)

[tab][ch]C#m7[/ch]    [ch]F#7[/ch]

[tab][ch]C#m7[/ch]                      [ch]F#7[/ch]

[tab][ch]C#m7[/ch]                  [ch]F#7[/ch]
From New York to Hollywood[/tab]
[tab][ch]C#m7[/ch]                    [ch]F#7[/ch]
It's ladies night and girl[/tab]
the feeling's good

(*Repeat)

This is your night, baby[/tab]

You've got to be there[/tab]
[tab][ch]Cm7[/ch]     [ch]Dm7[/ch]          [ch]Gm7[/ch]
Come on let's all celebrate[/tab]
[tab][ch]Cm7[/ch]                   [ch]Dm7[/ch]        [ch]Gm7[/ch]

| //// //// | /// /// /  |[/tab]


Bass Riff x4

[ch]F#7[/ch]
